file_insights.purpose: Preset loader with registry and name mapping functionality
key_components: _load_presets() - private loader function that reads JSON files and validates
list_presets() - public function to retrieve all presets
get_preset(name) - public function with hyphen/underscore name normalization
_PRESETS_DIR - module-level constant resolving to project root presets directory

patterns_discovered.name: On-demand Loading Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Presets are loaded from disk each time they are requested via _load_presets(), avoiding upfront initialization
patterns_discovered.location: lines 17-24, called by list_presets() and get_preset()

patterns_discovered.name: Name Normalization for CLI
patterns_discovered.description: Handles both hyphen and underscore variants by attempting direct lookup first, then trying the alternative form
patterns_discovered.location: lines 44-46, replaces hyphens with underscores or vice versa

patterns_discovered.name: Pydantic Model Validation
patterns_discovered.description: Uses Preset.model_validate() to ensure JSON data conforms to schema

patterns_discovered.name: Helpful Error Messages
patterns_discovered.description: KeyError includes list of available presets to guide users

gotchas_discovered.description: Each call to list_presets() or get_preset() triggers a full directory scan and JSON parsing. No caching mechanism implemented, leading to redundant file operations.
If presets/ directory doesn't exist, the function silently returns an empty dict without logging or warning.
The hyphen-underscore mapping handles only binary conversion (hyphen->underscore or underscore->hyphen). Edge cases like multiple consecutive separators or mixed cases are not addressed.

discoveries.approach_summary: Implementation successfully delivered all required functionality: JSON loading from presets/, Pydantic validation, preset registry with list/get operations, and bidirectional name mapping for CLI compatibility. Code is clean, well-documented, and follows Python conventions with private helper functions.

recommendations.suggestion: Implement caching using functools.lru_cache on _load_presets() or maintain a module-level cached dictionary to avoid repeated file I/O on subsequent calls
Add logging (warnings) when presets directory doesn't exist or when JSON parsing/validation fails, to aid in debugging
Add unit tests covering edge cases: missing directory, invalid JSON, validation failures, and various name normalization scenarios
Consider exposing _load_presets() as public (remove underscore) if external code needs to refresh the registry without importing private functions
Extend name normalization to handle additional transformations (camelCase, whitespace, etc.) if broader CLI compatibility is needed
